risks.mitigation: 14-day rolling average; don't use for primary decisions

charters.title: TRT-002 In Silico Design Pipeline
charters.phase: Track 1 (TRT-002a, ProteinMPNN-engineered follistatin fragment) computational pipeline COMPLETE. Track 2 (de novo ActRIIB miniprotein blocker) spun out to Charter 506. All 10 deliverables (D1-D10) closed.
charters.objective: Two-track drug design pipeline for TRT-002. Track 1: Engineered follistatin fragment (ND-FSD1-FSD2, 212 aa canine) as myostatin/activin antagonist. Track 2: De novo dual ActRIIA/ActRIIB competitive blocker (bimagrumab MOA) designed from Morvan crystal structures (2.0Å).
charters.successCriteria: ELISA IC50 < 100 nM myostatin inhibition = GO. Gene synthesis order placed, binding data in 6 weeks.

outstanding.text: Phase 6: Expert panel review (Hypatia + Socrates) — GO WITH CONDITIONS, all met
Track 2: Dual ActRII blocker design — review complete, BindCraft execution pending
Track 2: Execute BindCraft campaigns on Colab
Track 2: Resolve final conditions from panel → v4 final → BindCraft execution
Download BindCraft R1 PDB/CSV outputs from Colab (backed up to Drive)
Panel triage of 23 R1 designs → 9 advancing from 5 scaffolds
AF3 server validation of top 9 BindCraft R1 designs (in progress)
David selects final candidates for gene synthesis
Track 2: AF3 binary validation — 9/9 pass, controls establish dynamic range (0.11-0.84)
Track 2: Panel review of AF3 report — Hypatia GO, Socrates GO WITH CONDITIONS (folded in)
Track 2: Structural discrimination — 6-gate framework, 4 Tier 1 candidates from 2 scaffold families (s404474, s102094)
Track 2: Day 2 AF3 — re-seed top designs + ternary fold

decisions.text: Construct A (ND-FSD1-FSD2, 212 aa) is lead drug candidate
E. coli SHuffle T7 primary, Pichia backup
V7 robust (N82Q+R140Q+R169Q) strongest canine variant
PRODIGY validated for this target system
Ship imperfect drug — fix with data after ELISA proof
Sequence corrected: 215aa human → 212aa canine backbone
AF3 validates TRT-002a matches WT follistatin control
Expert panel GO WITH CONDITIONS — all conditions resolved
Track 2 (BindCraft de novo) approved in parallel
Track 2 pivoted: myostatin-specific binder → dual ActRII competitive blocker (bimagrumab MOA)
Design basis: Morvan et al. PNAS 2017 crystal structures (ActRIIA 2.35Å, ActRIIB 2.00Å)
Canine ActRIIB ECD = 100% identical to human (116/116 aa) — zero species translation risk
Charter 703 Track 2 review complete: Hypatia CONDITIONAL GO, Socrates IMPROVED/HIGH-RISK
BindCraft R1: 23 accepted de novo ActRIIB binder designs from ~60 trajectories (36% acceptance rate), lengths 80-120 aa
Big funnel strategy: maximize designs → confirmed binders → optimize from data (David's direction)
Panel triage complete: 9 designs from 5 scaffolds advance to AF3 validation. Top: l114_s404474_mpnn3 (i_pTM 0.83, dG -63.7). Best compact: l83_s36931_mpnn9 (83 aa, 1 Met)
AF3 validation 9/9 PASS — ipTM range 0.75-0.87 (mean 0.82). Best: s666280_mpnn20 (ipTM 0.87)
AF3 controls confirm gate validity: N1 scrambled ipTM 0.11 (floor), N2 rejected design ipTM 0.79 (ipTM alone insufficient), P3 bimagrumab Fv ipTM 0.84 (gold standard)
ActRIIB selectivity confirmed: lead design vs BMPRII ipTM 0.49 — addresses ACE-031 off-target safety concern

Structural discrimination complete: 4 Tier 1 from 2 scaffold families (s404474, s102094). G5 (focused competitive-face contacts) is primary discriminator. N2 rejected design fails structural gates despite AF3 ipTM 0.79 — validates two-stage pipeline.

Fleet restructure: Morpheus + Archimedes stay Opus, Aristotle + Arete move to Sonnet, Daedalus + Hermes stay GPT-5.4 (free)

risks.mitigation: Aggressive compaction, shorter sessions, fewer heartbeats

Validate NAC removal improves VYS outcomes in next protocol run

decisions.text: VYS swelling identified as primary failure mode
NAC paradox: antioxidant suppresses ROS-dependent vascular signaling (HIF-1α, VEGF)
Drop NAC, keep ITS-X — metabolic support without vascular harm

charters.title: Longform Task Harness
charters.phase: Code is present on the OpenClaw machine; provider configured as codex-cli with gpt-5.5; ~/.codex/auth.json exists in chatgpt mode; voice corpus loaded; automated tests pass; multiple BioWire Byte harness runs exist with FINAL.md/HEADLINES.md artifacts. Remaining work is quality/prompt iteration and David publishability approval.
charters.objective: Build a Python harness that orchestrates LLMs through multistep science/writing workflows the same way Claude Code orchestrates Opus through software engineering. Externalized state, scoped per-phase prompts, deterministic + LLM-as-judge validators, retry/correction loops, resumable runs.
charters.successCriteria: harness run biowire-byte --topic '<bio topic>' produces a publishable BioWire Byte newsletter post end-to-end with zero human intervention beyond initial topic input. Mid-run kill + resume works cleanly. All citations verified to real primary sources. Voice match ≥7/10. <15 min wall clock per run.

Spec uploaded to Google Docs, shared with David
Hand spec to Claude Code, kick off build
Build steps 1-4 — skeleton, schemas, state.py, templates.py, context.py
Build step 5 — OpenAICodexClient with tool-calling support
Build steps 6-7 — deterministic validators, research tools (pubmed/arxiv/web_fetch/doi)
Build step 8 — runner.py state machine loop
Build step 9 — LLM-as-judge validators with rubrics
End-to-end test: real BioWire Byte run on GPT-5.5
David approves first AI-generated BioWire Byte as publishable
v2 deferred work: Anthropic provider adapter, deep dive pipeline
Transfer code from build machine to OpenClaw via GitHub repo
Fix Codex auth.json parser — actual file is ~/.codex/auth.json with auth_mode field
Load David's 21+ existing BioWire Bytes into voice_corpus/
Iterate on prompts/*.txt with real LLM responses
Resolve OpenAI auth for runtime: API key, ChatGPT OAuth via codex login, or local Ollama fallback
v2 deferred work: Anthropic provider adapter / alternate-model judging, deep dive pipeline

decisions.text: Build with Claude Code (free via Max), runtime LLM = GPT-5.5 via OAuth (free)
Python over Node — best LLM tooling, Pydantic for schemas
One pipeline in v1: BioWire Byte. Deep dive + paper critique deferred to v2/v3
STATE.json canonical, STATE.md regenerated for humans (Dozer fix)
Tool-calling baked into LLMClient protocol from day one (Dozer fix)
Critics run sequentially in v1, not parallel (Dozer fix — avoids surprise async architecture)
Same-model judge limitation accepted in v1, threshold lowered to 7/10 (Dozer fix)
Pluggable provider design, but only OpenAI Codex implemented in v1 — no placeholder files
Daedalus owns build; Morpheus owns spec + dashboard tracking
Built on separate machine (kingsley_home), transferring via GitHub repo davemkingsley-byte/longform-harness
Codex auth path corrected: ~/.codex/auth.json (not oauth.json), branches on auth_mode (apikey vs chatgpt)
Voice corpus seeded with 9 published-quality BioWire Bytes (019-025 + specials)

risks: GPT-5.5 spiralist vulnerability — mitigated by tightly scoped per-phase prompts; harness never forwards freeform user input
Same-model LLM-as-judge produces inflated scores — accepted v1 limitation, plan to use Anthropic in v2 for judging
Voice matching may be insufficient on first run — fallback is manual edit + retry; David's 21+ existing posts are the corpus
Tool-calling complexity could blow up scope — mitigated by explicit protocol design and v1 scope to 4 tools only

milestones.label: Skeleton + state machine + provider adapter compiling
